steps.title: Identify the missing element
steps.content: The subject is Louisiana resident Oscar James Dunn, with a long appositive phrase ("one of the nearly two thousand African Americans...Civil War") interrupting before the verb. The sentence still needs a finite verb to complete the main clause.

steps.title: Choose the finite verb
steps.content:
began is the only finite (past-tense) verb among the choices. To begin is an infinitive, having begun is a participle, and beginning is also a participle — none can stand as the main verb.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
